an8.66:1.1:
1
"Bhikkhus, there are these eight emancipations.
What eight?
an8.66:1.2: (1) "One possessing form sees forms. This is the first emancipation.
an8.66:1.3:
2
(2) "One not percipient of forms internally sees forms externally. This is the second emancipation.
an8.66:1.4:
3
(3) "One is focused only on ‘beautiful.’ This is the third emancipation.
an8.66:1.5:
4
(4) "With the complete surmounting of perceptions of forms, with the passing away of perceptions of sensory impingement, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity,
‘space is infinite,’ one enters and dwells in the base of the infinity of space. This is the fourth emancipation.
an8.66:1.6:
5
(5) "By completely surmounting the base of the infinity of space,
‘consciousness is infinite,’ one enters and dwells in the base of the infinity of consciousness. This is the fifth emancipation.
an8.66:1.7:
6
(6) "By completely surmounting the base of the infinity of consciousness,
‘there is nothing,’ one enters and dwells in the base of nothingness. This is the sixth emancipation.
an8.66:1.8:
7
(7) "By completely surmounting the base of nothingness, one enters and dwells in the base of neither-perception-nor-non-perception. This is the seventh emancipation.
an8.66:1.9:
8
(8)"By completely surmounting the base of neither-perception-nor-non-perception, one enters and dwells in the cessation of perception and feeling.
This is the eighth emancipation.
